content: CLAUDE.md encodes core pebblr invariants: secrets via file mounts (never env vars), Web Components only (no framework), per-row RBAC enforced at data layer, Makefile convention (CI calls make targets only, multi-step logic in scripts/)
Project layout uses web/ (not frontend/) for TypeScript/Web Components and deploy/helm/ (not charts/) for Helm 4 charts

All Web Component custom elements use pbl- prefix (e.g. <pbl-lead-card>). Class names are Pbl + PascalCase. API endpoints under /api/v1/.... Error responses: {"error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}}. Go errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf("doing X: %w", err). No global state; deps via constructors. List endpoints support pagination (?page=, ?limit=) and filtering.

name: new-component-skill
description: new-component skill (.claude/commands/new-component.md) scaffolds pbl-prefixed Web Components with component file + test file, PascalCase class, static readonly tag, and connectedCallback pattern

content: Frontend is React + TypeScript: functional components (.tsx), Vite bundler, TanStack Query for server state, TanStack Table for data grids, Vitest + React Testing Library for tests. No Web Components, no class components, no global state managers.
Makefile targets delegate to scripts/: each target is a one-liner or calls scripts/<name>.sh. Multi-step logic never lives directly in Makefile recipes.

Helm chart uses secretMountPath pattern: all secrets delivered via ExternalSecret CRD → Kubernetes Secret → volume mount at /run/secrets/. No env vars for secrets. ExternalSecret references Azure KeyVault via ESO SecretStore.

Go package layout: internal/domain (types), internal/auth (OIDC), internal/rbac (policy+context), internal/events (event log), internal/metrics (telemetry), internal/store (repo interfaces), internal/store/postgres (pgx impls), internal/api (HTTP). All interfaces defined in internal/store and internal/events; postgres impls are placeholders.

RBAC is dual-layer: application layer (rbac.Enforcer) + PostgreSQL RLS (defense-in-depth). RLS policies use session-local vars app.user_id and app.user_role set per request. Three roles: rep (own leads), manager (team leads), admin (all).

Database migrations use golang-migrate format (migrate:up / migrate:down comments) in migrations/ directory. Migration runner at cmd/migrate/main.go reads DSN from mounted file (never env var). First migration (001_initial_schema.sql) creates users, teams, customers, leads, lead_events tables + RLS policies.
